There are a lot of lies going around about the situation of water in Gaza, let's set the record straight.

Across Gaza, the water supply consistently exceeds the humanitarian threshold.

Total daily delivery breakdown:

Birkat Said and Bani Suheila lines: Daily average               14, 000 cubic meters each.
Emirati line: Over 5,000 cubic meters daily.
Deir Al Balah & Southern desalination: Around 18,000 cubic meters daily.

Dozens of wells and water pumping facilities operate across Gaza, supplying tens of thousands of cubic meters a day.

Over 70,000 cubic meters of water are facilitated daily.

Despite the complexities of the security environment and Hamas's constant attempts to weaponize aid for terror purposes, we remain committed to fueling local pumping facilities and ensuring that clean water reaches civilians.

R to @DXBMediaOffice: Mohammed bin Rashid: UAE continues to strengthen its position as a global hub for advanced food industries through innovation, stronger production chains, and boosting the competitiveness of national products. The dates industry is a successful example of turning the country’s agricultural heritage into a modern, high-value added sector that supports the economy and enhances food security.

Mohammed bin Rashid visits Al Barakah Dates Factory,  the world’s largest privately owned dates factory at Dubai Industrial City. Spanning over 800,000 square feet, the facility has an annual production capacity of 100,000 tonnes and stands as a global leader in date processing and packaging, with exports reaching 97 countries worldwide.

His Excellency Mohammed bin Mubarak bin Fadhel Al Mazrouei, Minister of State for Defence Affairs, met His Excellency Alistair Carns, Minister for the UK Armed Forces, and his accompanying delegation.

During the meeting, the two sides discussed military and defence cooperation and ways to enhance coordination and mutual understanding in areas of common interest, to support regional and international security and stability.

They underscored the importance of continued exchange of expertise and the development of defence partnerships to strengthen armed forces capabilities and address evolving security challenges.

Let’s talk facts, not slogans. Since the ceasefire began, over 12,500 tons of medical equipment have entered Gaza. Israel places no limits on medical aid, and we have approved every single request for medication since the war began.

This includes the human element: We coordinate with our partners the movements of medical teams in and out of Gaza, with hundreds working on the ground.

Our security screenings exist for one reason: to keep aid out of the hands of Hamas. When an item is flagged, we work with our international partners to find an alternative solution. The results are visible to everyone on the ground.

Since the humanitarian pause began, we have facilitated the entry of over 12,500 tons of medical supplies and 20 ambulances to reinforce local healthcare capacity.

Today, 12 field hospitals and 11 permanent hospitals are operational across Gaza. Within these facilities, medical teams,including hundreds of international specialists,work to provide vital care to those who need it most.

We work with the international community to expand healthcare access. However, this mission faces a constant challenge: Hamas’s ongoing use of hospitals and medical infrastructure for its own terror porpuses. Despite these complexities, we remain committed to ensuring that medical aid reaches Gazan civilians and that the healthcare system continues to function.

🎯DISMANTLED: An underground terror tunnel containing living quarters, military equipment and weapons.

IDF troops dismantled an offensive underground route ~800 meters long, which contained living quarters, military equipment and weapons, including vests and a rocket belonging to Islamic Jihad.

600 trucks on average. Every single day.

Whether via the UN or the private sector, we are moving the goods that Gaza needs. With 70-80% of these trucks carrying food, we are ensuring a steady and reliable flow of supplies directly to local markets.

The facts speak for themselves: there is no limit to the amount of aid that can enter. While some attempt to claim otherwise, the reality on the ground is a consistent, long-term stream of humanitarian and commercial goods that remains stable day after day.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met and held talks with Sultan of Oman Haytham bin Tariq in the Baraka Palace of Muscat on Sunday to discuss bilateral ties and recent developments in the region. pic.

Since the ceasefire began, we have facilitated the entry of 1.5 million tons of food into Gaza, specifically prioritizing essential proteins and dietary fiber to maintain public health.

On the ground, this translates to stable, daily solutions: 209 local kitchens are currently providing hot meals, while 33 bakeries produce 3.6 million fresh pita loaves for Gazan families every single day.

Our mission is clear: we work in full coordination with the UN and international organizations to meet their requests when it comes to the needs of the population. There is no shortage. Resources are available, now and for the long term.
